description:
You are helping a technical-writer who wants to preview a set of HTML files locally before publishing them.  
The writer already has the documentation stored in:

/home/user/docs/

Goal  
1. Launch a lightweight web server that serves the content of /home/user/docs on TCP port 8765.  
2. Make sure the server keeps running in the background (do NOT block the terminal).  
3. Capture the server’s initial console banner in a log file located at:  
   /home/user/logs/docs_webserver.log

Log-file requirements  
• The log file must be created if it does not exist.  
• Its first non-empty line must follow this exact literal pattern (no extra spaces, case-sensitive):  
  Serving HTTP on 0.0.0.0 port 8765  
• Any subsequent output produced by the web server should also be appended to that same file.

Verification hints (what the automatic grader will check)  
• A process listening on port 8765 exists and is serving /home/user/docs.  
• /home/user/logs/docs_webserver.log exists, is not empty, and its first non-empty line is precisely:  
  Serving HTTP on 0.0.0.0 port 8765  
• The web server process must continue running after your command returns (i.e. it is in the background).

Remember  
– Stay entirely in the user’s home directory tree; no sudo is available.  
– Do not kill the web server once it is launched—leave it running for the grader to inspect.  
– Use only standard utilities that are already present on a minimal Linux installation (e.g., Python’s built-in http.server module).
truth:
Initial state before the agent begins: 1. The directory /home/user/docs/ exists and contains at least the file /home/user/docs/index.html with the single line:

Technical Documentation Home

2. The directory /home/user/logs/ exists and is writable by the user. The file /home/user/logs/docs_webserver.log does NOT exist yet. 3. No process is listening on port 8765 initially. Expected final state for the verifier: 1. A background process is running whose command line begins exactly: python -m http.server 8765 and whose working directory is /home/user/docs (verifiable via /proc/<pid>/cwd). 2. Port 8765 is in LISTEN state for IPv4 (0.0.0.0:8765). 3. /home/user/logs/docs_webserver.log exists and its first non-empty line is exactly: Serving HTTP on 0.0.0.0 port 8765 (A trailing newline is allowed.) All subsequent stdout/stderr from the server may follow in the same file. 4. The python process must still be alive when checks happen (approx. 10–15 s after task completion).
name: task_000000_b2dab9c4
